bug_b_resolution.summary: Split base.ts into inline ViewPlugin + block StateField paths. DecorationFamily declares its kind. Composer routes accordingly. Also fixed an overlap-shape bug in headings (mark covering replace range silently dropped) by structuring the heading mark to start after the marker. _helpers.ts hideMarker now uses an explicit empty WidgetType for defense-in-depth against the same overlap class.

gap_closure_followup.note: Tests added to frontend/tests/e2e/live-preview/live-preview.spec.ts (18 tests total). PENDING execution; --run-tests would elevate the new test_passes evidence to VERIFIED and convergence likely to CONVERGED for all invariants.

passed: S2: embedded <script> and onerror handlers stay inert
run_tests_iteration.root_cause_summary: Two distinct bugs surfaced during execution. Bug A (one-line, pre-existing latent) blocked decoration plugin construction once any wiki-link source was on the page. After Bug A was fixed and binary rebuilt, Bug B (architectural) replaced it: CodeMirror's ViewPlugin contract forbids block-level decorations, but base.ts emits Decoration.line and Decoration.replace({block:true}) for blockquote / fenced-code / tables families through the same ViewPlugin as inline. Browser throws 'RangeError: Block decorations may not be specified via plugins' at first build, no decorations get applied, all surface-rendering tests fail because no .cm-md-* classes exist in the DOM.

bugs_found.description: String.prototype.replaceAll(/^\\/+/, '') used a non-global regex; ECMA spec requires global flag, V8 throws TypeError. Code was migrated as-is from the pre-existing Editor.svelte::normalizeWikiName helper, where it was latent until any source containing wiki-link syntax landed in the visible viewport.
bugs_found.fix: Changed replaceAll to replace (regex is anchored, semantically equivalent).

bugs_found.description: liveDecorationPlugin() builds a single ViewPlugin that emits decorations from all families. CM6 forbids block-level decorations from ViewPlugins — they must come from a StateField via EditorView.decorations.from(field). Three of the V1 families produce block decorations: blockquote (Decoration.line), fenced-code (Decoration.line), tables (Decoration.replace({block: true})). At runtime CM throws RangeError before any decorations apply, so the entire decoration system silently fails.
bugs_found.fix_proposal: Refactor base.ts to two paths: keep ViewPlugin for inline families (gives viewport-scoped + selectionSet update), add a StateField for block families (full-doc scope, doc-change update). Family declares its kind via DecorationFamily interface. Composer routes accordingly. ~50-100 lines of refactor; existing inline families need no change.

gaps.issue: No explicit assertion that the editor surface is single (no split pane, no second CodeMirror instance)
gaps.action: Add a Playwright assertion: `await expect(page.locator('.cm-editor')).toHaveCount(1)` to live-preview.spec.ts (or wiki/crud.spec.ts)
gaps.rationale: B1 is structurally upheld by the codebase shape; absence of a test for 'thing not present' is a low-priority gap.

gaps.issue: No e2e test that types into the live-preview editor, saves, and asserts the persisted content equals the raw markdown source (no rendered HTML in autosave POST)
gaps.action: Add a test in live-preview.spec.ts: type a heading + bold + table into the editor, observe network POST to /api/pages/<n>, assert request body's `content` field is byte-identical to the typed markdown source
gaps.rationale: T1 is the most load-bearing invariant — if a decoration ever mutates state.doc, autosave corrupts the wiki. The structural code-review finding is strong but a direct round-trip test is the canonical T1 gate.

gaps.issue: T2 carries a statistical threshold (p95 < 16ms) but no perf benchmark exists
gaps.action: Add a one-off Node-runnable benchmark under scripts/ (similar to verify-rt1-gfm-parsing.mjs) that constructs an EditorView with the live-preview extension over a fixture document, exercises the rebuild path N times, and reports p50/p95/p99 latencies. Optional: bake into make bundle-size or a sibling make perf target
gaps.rationale: Statistical Verification check flags this. Code is structured to honour the budget (viewport-scoped + debounced + memoized) but the actual distribution is unmeasured.

gaps.issue: live-preview.spec.ts asserts cursor-OUT renders decorated forms but never asserts cursor-IN reveals raw source
gaps.action: Extend each family test in live-preview.spec.ts: after the cursor-out assertion, position cursor inside the construct (e.g. via `page.locator('.cm-content').click({ position: ... })`) and assert raw markdown source becomes visible (e.g. `**bold**` text node present)
gaps.rationale: U1 is the defining UX contract of SilverBullet-style live preview. Without cursor-in tests, regressions could ship that make the editor un-editable.

gaps.issue: No test asserts that plain typing does not move the cursor or scroll the viewport
gaps.action: Add a test: position cursor at end of a paragraph, capture state.selection.main.head + scrollDOM.scrollTop via page.evaluate, type a single 'a', assert new head = old head + 1 and scrollTop unchanged

gaps.issue: No test asserts that markdown constructs not in U2 (footnotes, math, raw HTML embeds) render as raw source unchanged
gaps.action: Add a test with a fixture containing `[^footnote-ref]` and footnote definition, assert no .cm-md-* class is applied to the footnote markup and the raw `[^...]` text is visible

gaps.issue: live-preview.spec.ts covers `javascript:` collapsing but not data:, vbscript:, file:, about:
gaps.action: Extend the markdown-links test in live-preview.spec.ts with a fixture containing each unsafe scheme and assert href=='#' for all of them. Same coverage already exists at server side in internal/render/markdown_test.go; this is the editor-side parity check
gaps.rationale: S1 is invariant; missing scheme classes are a real attack surface. Editor-side and server-side allow-lists must demonstrate parity in tests.

gaps.issue: No test that injects raw HTML / `<script>` into markdown source and asserts it stays inert in the rendered widgets
gaps.action: Add a test with a fixture containing `**<script>window.__pwned=1</script>**` and `[link](http://x)<script>...</script>`, assert (a) no `<script>` element exists inside the editor, (b) `window.__pwned` remains undefined
gaps.rationale: S2 is the textContent-only invariant. Code review confirms current widgets are clean, but a regression test prevents future regressions from a careless `el.innerHTML =` slipping in.

new_blocking_gap_g9.issue: Block decorations cannot be emitted from a ViewPlugin (CM6 contract). base.ts puts inline + block families behind one ViewPlugin. Surfaced by --run-tests. See run_tests_iteration.bugs_found[BUG-B] for full description and proposed fix.
